description: Primeiro Grupo Catarinense (PGC) is the largest criminal organization in the state of Santa Catarina, Brazil, founded on March 3, 2003, in the Florianópolis prison. It commands rebellions, robberies, kidnappings, murders, and drug trafficking, operating primarily in Santa Catarina with extensions into at least three other Brazilian states and borders with Paraguay, Bolivia, and Peru. The group has 14 leaders divided into two ministries and is modeled after the PCC and Comando Vermelho.

country_links.evidence_quote: Território(s): Santa Catarina (principalmente), elos em Paraná , Mato Grosso , Mato Grosso do Sul , Paraguai , Bolívia e Peru .
country_links.context: The organization has links in Bolivia, indicating a footprint through routing or supply.
